result.summary: The page is mostly usable, but several horizontally scrollable elements appear clipped at the right edge, making the layout look partially broken. There are also a few minor spacing and readability issues caused by dense fixed navigation and overlays.

issues.description: Game cards in multiple sections are cut off on the right side of the viewport. The third card in each row is only partially visible, which can look like the page content is overflowing or broken rather than intentionally scrollable.
issues.suggestion: If these are horizontal carousels, add clearer affordance such as consistent card peeking, a fade gradient, scroll indicators, or arrows. If not intended, constrain the grid to the viewport and adjust card widths/gaps for mobile.

issues.description: The top category navigation is clipped on the right, with the 'Hot Ga...' tab only partially visible. This makes the text look broken and may confuse users.
issues.suggestion: Ensure the horizontal nav has proper scroll behavior and visual cues, or resize tabs so labels are not abruptly cut off. Consider adding a fade at the edge to indicate more content.

issues.description: The fixed bottom navigation overlaps the lower part of the game grid area, cutting into the visible content near the bottom of the screen.
issues.suggestion: Add bottom padding to the page content equal to the height of the fixed navigation so the last row of cards can scroll fully above it.

issues.description: Some small overlay elements on game cards, such as the info icons and jackpot amounts, sit on busy image backgrounds and may be difficult to read.
issues.suggestion: Use stronger contrast treatments, larger touch targets, or more consistent dark overlays behind text/icons.
